story.community_prompt: Which nature topic should Wild Brief explain next after storm?
story.cta_prompt: Want to see more extreme weather explained fast?
story.description: A clip of weather, clouds, storms, lightning, snow, or sky phenomena: vibrant purple electric lightning animation
story.hook: This storm changes what happens next
story.lead: Purple lightning paints the sky. Notice how the bolt isn't just white—it’s glowing purple. That’s because it’s supercharging nitrogen in the air, splitting molecules that split again in light. That’s why storms sometimes turn violet. Ever seen one like this?

story.community_prompt: Which nature topic should Wild Brief explain next after plant?
story.cta_prompt: Want more fast nature science that actually connects?
story.description: A clip of plants, leaves, flowers, or plant movement: close up of vibrant amaryllis flowers
story.lead: They color-shift all day—start blue, end red. Watch the petals’ edges darken first. Because the sap’s pH rises with sunlight, turning pigments from cool to warm. Notice how the deep veins stay dark the whole time. That’s why the color crawls like paint on wet paper. Ever seen a plant do time-lapse in real life.
